top_signals.title: H2 OUTLOOK: Shipping bottlenecks, uncertainty to impact Middle East chemical trade
top_signals.summary: Petrochemical supply flows from the Middle East over the second half of the year are contingent on shipping bottlenecks, many of which will depend on how the conflict between the US and Iran develops. But, market participants said, it’s unlikely there will be swift return to prewar trade flow levels for most chemicals originating in ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Crude Oil, Gasoline Inventories Still Falling
top_signals.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 564,000 barrels in the week ending July 10. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 399,000 barrels. Although commercial crude oil inventories excluding the SPR have been falling rapidly for three months now, shedding just over 60 million barrels over the last twelve weeks, US crude inventories are only down 9.2 million barrels so far this year, according to API data, kept in check by draws from the SPR. For the week ending July…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Svitzer bunkers first London harbour tug with HVO 100
top_signals.summary: Towage provider Svitzer has bunkered its harbour tug Svitzer Thames with 100% Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil (HVO 100) at London Gateway. The operation marks the first time one of the company’s London-based harbour tugs has been fuelled with the renewable diesel. The company said the switch from conventional marine diesel to HVO 100 can significantly reduce ...

top_signals.title: Traffic via Strait of Hormuz falls to 11 ships July 12 after US-Iran attacks
top_signals.summary: Only 11 ships traversed the Strait of Hormuz on July 12, the lowest since June 14, after attacks hit the container ship GFS Galaxy and the US and Iran made retaliatory strikes, S&P Global Commodities at Sea(opens in a new tab) said in a July 13 report. The total included one LNG tanker, one general ...

top_signals.title: China Economy: New action plan opens more opportunities for foreign investors
top_signals.summary: China is rolling out new measures to attract and stabilize foreign investment, with a focus on opening more service sectors and improving conditions for overseas businesses. The move comes as foreign investment flows show signs of becoming more innovation-driven. Bi Ran reports from Shanghai. In the first half of 2026 alone, more than a dozen ...

top_signals.title: ASML rises 4% after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
top_signals.summary: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Up for 4th Day
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, was up for the fourth session on Tuesday, rising about 0.7% to a new high since June 5 at 2,980 points, driven by gains across all vessel segments. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore ...

top_signals.title: Newbuilding Activity Going Strong
top_signals.summary: Newbuilding activity has remained strong throughout the past week. In its latest weekly report, shipbroker Banchero Costa said that “newbuilding activity remained firm this week, with ordering interest led by the dry bulk sector, while notable contracts were also concluded across the container, car carrier, tanker and gas carrier segments.
